sweep_metadata.notes: Day 83 priority scope concentrates on the May 20-21 compressed decision window. Five fired triggers this cycle: (1) Vahidi direct named X post (devastating-blows formulation) - the long-monitored apex Iranian-principal silent vertex breaks; (2) Knesset dissolution preliminary vote PASSED 110-0 with Netanyahu absent at security consultations - the Day 82 BS-3 framework-revision trigger fires; (3) UAE official attribution of Barakah drones to IRAQI TERRITORY (not Yemen) - reshapes proxy-network reading and Cooper T1 discount mechanism; (4) Vance new principal-vertex active in PA-gap architecture ('locked and loaded' May 19; 'pretty good spot' May 20) - intra-Republican faction dynamics operationally visible per BS-17; (5) Trump extends 2-3 day window to 'Friday Saturday Sunday early next week' (May 19) - Gulf state troika brake holds, decision window extended approximately to May 22-25. Concurrent: WPR resolution discharged from committee May 19 (Vance day; advances toward 8th vote without yet voting on merits); Brent at $111.28 (May 19), pulled back marginally from above-band position but not into the $102-110 oscillation band; Mojtaba X corpus 'Third Sacred Defense' rhetoric flagged as Tier-3 (NOT Tier-1 Mahdist invocation); Pezeshkian 'dialogue does not mean surrender' (May 19, X) consistent with Day 81 framing. Thesis holding with three required revisions queued: (a) Vahidi statement crystallizes BS-12 Vahidi-shaped PA-gap; (b) Knesset dissolution-trigger activation; (c) UAE Iraqi-territory attribution refines BS-13 proxy-network assessment.

sources.name: Fox News liveblog May 20 2026 - 'IRGC commander Vahidi: any further aggression will erupt into flames and transcend every border... devastating blows that will leave you in abject defeat'
Iranian President Pezeshkian X post May 19 2026 - 'Dialogue does not mean surrender... will never bow our heads before the enemy'
Hot Air / Reuters digest May 19 2026 - Iran 'new proposal' framed by US media as maximalist; 14-point text content gap with prior US proposal

finding_cards.finding: FRAMEWORK REVISION TRIGGER. Maj. Gen. Ahmad Vahidi (named per Fox News liveblog May 20, attributed to X post) issued direct statement: 'If any further aggression is committed against the soil of Iran, that fire whose promise was previously given and remained confined within the framework of a limited regional war, this time will erupt into flames and transcend every border and domain; you will receive devastating blows that will leave you in abject defeat.' This is the first direct named Vahidi public statement on the conflict captured by the framework's source ladder since the Day 70 audit elevated PROBE-2 watch to the Vahidi-direct-quote signal. The framing is hardline deterrent (capability + intent) not direct deal-rejection or 5-precondition reaffirmation; opposes Israeli/US kinetic resumption but does not address MOU terms substantively. Pezeshkian X post May 19 'dialogue does not mean surrender' parallels Vahidi tone (defiance + non-rejection of dialogue). Apex-mid-tier split structurally preserved: Ghalibaf-Araghchi pro-deal track operative (Pakistan-channel revised text); Vahidi apex now publicly visible as deterrent-hardline-without-explicit-MOU-stance. Aliabadi silent. PROBE-3 13th gap. Note: identification convergence required - the framework's prior characterization (per ISW April 21/May 12, v4.0 §5.23) is of Vahidi as 'apex decision-maker' in the IRGC consolidation; the Fox News byline identifies the same Vahidi as 'IRGC commander'; both refer to Maj. Gen. Ahmad Vahidi (former Defense Minister, post-Twelve-Day-War IRGC apex per ISW). Discount applied: Fox News liveblog single-source on the X-post attribution; -25%.

finding_cards.conflict_notes: Vahidi hardline-deterrent framing vs concurrent Pakistan-channel revised-text submission and Pezeshkian non-rejection-of-dialogue: not contradiction; consistent with the two-level-game pattern observed across the cycle (public deterrence + private diplomatic engagement). The framework should not over-weight the deterrence framing as MOU-rejection; the apex signal is bounded to kinetic-pathway threshold, not terms-substantive.

CNBC May 16 2026 - Trump-Xi Day 2 readout - Xi confirms Hormuz must remain open; no direct mediation activation since

finding_cards.finding: Chinese principal-level silence holds since Trump-Xi Day 2 communique (May 15). Al Jazeera May 20 reports Chinese supertankers EXITING Hormuz concurrent with Trump-Vance deal optimism rhetoric - operationally significant maritime-flow signal but ambiguous between (a) commercial pre-positioning ahead of expected deal completion (deal-confidence signal), (b) protective withdrawal anticipating possible kinetic resumption (hedging signal), or (c) routine cargo cycle. No new Xi statement, no Wang Yi-Araghchi readout. Beijing's mediating channel substantively dormant this cycle; Pakistan-channel architecture (Sharif/Munir + Tasnim 14-point text routing) remains the operative Iranian diplomatic conduit. No new OFAC designations of Chinese entities. No Taiwan Strait PLA spike. BeiDou-3/MizarVision sub-components remain structurally opaque. Xi's Day 1 substantive commitments (Hormuz open; no nuclear weapons; no military equipment; offered mediation) remain the operative Chinese principal-level position carried forward from Day 77 baseline.

Ford returned home Norfolk May 16 (Day 81 baseline; correctly identified as CSG-10 departed, not Truman). No new ROE change. No DOE/SOF nuclear-expertise forward deployment signal. No new operation name. Trump Truth Social May 18 (T1): SecDef Hegseth and Joint Chiefs Chairman Cain instructed to 'be prepared to go forward with a full, large scale assault of Iran, on a moment's notice' - posture-language reaffirmed in May 19-20 framing ('locked and loaded' Vance) but no fresh operational signal. The 'scheduled attack' for May 19 was postponed; Gulf-state-brake mechanism holds; Trump extended window to 'Friday Saturday Sunday early next week' (May 19). The standing-attack-readiness state persists under the brake.

finding_cards.finding: Brent crude closed $111.28 (May 19), down 0.73% intraday on mixed Trump signals - PARTIAL retracement from the prior week's above-band position ($111+ Day 79) but NOT a reversion into the $102-110 A1-oscillation band. The Day 81 sweep's read of ~$102 reflected an intraday intra-cycle move; the May 19 close at $111.28 shows the deal-signal premium-compression did not durably hold. Net: kinetic-resumption-risk premium is reasserting at the cycle's close. Drivers: (1) Trump 'might have to give Iran another big hit' (May 19) and Vance 'locked and loaded' rhetoric - hardline framing reactivated; (2) US oil-sanctions waiver proposal (Tasnim/Bloomberg meta-T2) US-side unconfirmed; Treasury action this cycle was Russian-crude-tanker waiver (distinct policy); (3) Chinese supertanker exit Hormuz May 20 - signal-ambiguous (deal-confidence vs protective hedging). The oscillation band is structurally broken upward; $110-115 is the new operating range with kinetic-risk premium sustained. No commercial-restoration announcement (Hapag-Lloyd/Maersk). Hormuz IRGC physical enforcement maintained (Zolfaghari baseline). Brent through $130 in 60 days: holds at 30-40% range (Day 81 marker) pending May 20-21 window resolution; downside-to-Fork-B-trigger ($95) not approached this cycle.

trigger_detail.appendix_b_row: Brent breakout above $115 sustained (not fired - $111.28 close); Brent breakout below $95 (not fired); Kharg saturation (not fired); commercial restoration (not fired)

trigger_detail.direction: stable-elevated - above-band position ($110-115) holds; deal-premium-reversion of Day 81 did not durably hold; kinetic-risk premium reasserting
trigger_detail.gap_to_full_match: Sustained $115+ for Fork A repricing; $95 break for Fork B credibility; commercial restoration announcement

variable_moved.new_value: Day 83: Brent $111.28 (May 19 close); above-band position sustained; deal-premium reversal of Day 81 was intraday, not session-close-durable. Operating range $110-115 with kinetic-resumption premium sustained. The structural read: the band ($102-110) that held through pre-Barakah cycles is broken upward; pricing reflects kinetic-risk continuity even under Gulf-state brake.

finding_cards.conflict_notes: Day 81 sweep noted ~$102 intraday in deal-signal cluster; Day 83 close at $111.28 shows the reversion was not session-durable. The Day 81 reading was correctly flagged as 'partial' but the directional interpretation (band reversion) was over-weighted on intraday movement. Framework lesson: distinguish intraday-deal-signal-spikes from session-close band-position; price the latter.

sources.name: Haaretz May 20 2026 - 'Israel nears elections as Knesset passes preliminary dissolution bill'
Times of Israel May 20 2026 - 'MKs unanimously advance bill to dissolve Knesset' - 110-0 preliminary passage; 13 separate dissolution bills on plenum agenda
Jerusalem Post May 20 2026 - 'Coalition's bill to dissolve Knesset passes preliminary reading amid crisis over haredi draft bill'
Israel Hayom May 20 2026 - Netanyahu absent at security consultations; election countdown initiated
Fox News May 19 2026 - IDF on 'high alert'; Netanyahu emergency security meeting

finding_cards.finding: FRAMEWORK REVISION TRIGGER. Knesset preliminary dissolution vote PASSED 110-0 on May 20 (zero opposition). Netanyahu was ABSENT from the chamber, holding security consultations - the most consequential coalition-discipline failure in this conflict's window. Triggering event was the coalition's failure to pass the haredi-draft-exemption bill; Degel HaTorah spiritual leader Dov Lando instructed lawmakers to vote dissolution. The Day 82 Appendix B framework-revision trigger ('Knesset dissolution bill passes') fires; the BS-3 election-horizon-locks-mid-October mechanism is now operative. The Day 81 mechanism Day 79's 'all but assured' forecast is realized. Three additional Knesset readings required for full dissolution; passage of preliminary by unanimous vote effectively forecloses Netanyahu's capacity to delay. Election horizon: September-October 2026 (Haredi parties reportedly pushing September). The IDF-coalition uranium-removal asymmetry mechanism (Day 77 sub-finding: Zamir 'all goals achieved', senior IDF 'diplomatic removal acceptable') is now phase-relevant: post-dissolution caretaker period shifts operational-decision authority toward IDF leadership; pre-caretaker (current state, awaiting subsequent readings) Netanyahu retains operational authority. Netanyahu's absence from the vote is itself a Penetration-mechanism signal: Trump consultations (or strike-readiness consultations per Fox News) outweighed coalition-discipline imperatives. IDF on 'high alert' (Fox News May 19); security cabinet pre-May 18 ran ~5 hours; no Day 82-83 Israeli unilateral strike.

trigger_detail.direction: Israeli election horizon locks September-October 2026; Netanyahu pre-emption-incentive structurally elevated (electoral compression on coalition decision-window) until full dissolution; post-dissolution caretaker shifts authority toward IDF leadership (HEU-diplomatic-removal-accepting); pre-caretaker Netanyahu retains operational authority. Net: Fork A Israeli-variant 14-21d probability holds at Day 81 baseline (28-38%) but mechanism timing now phase-dependent on subsequent readings.
trigger_detail.gap_to_full_match: N/A - FIRED. Full dissolution (three more readings) is mechanical; preliminary unanimous passage forecloses Netanyahu delay capacity. Synthesis revision candidate.

IDF-coalition uranium-removal asymmetry mechanism enters phase-dependent activation: pre-caretaker Netanyahu retains authority; post-caretaker shifts toward IDF leadership. The trigger-table line 'Israeli election horizon locks ≤mid-Oct; Netanyahu pre-emption incentive structurally elevated' is now operative.

finding_cards.conflict_notes: No source conflict. The 110-0 unanimity is the most decisive Knesset signal in the conflict's window; even Netanyahu's own coalition lawmakers voted dissolution (the absent Netanyahu does not count). The structural reading: the haredi-draft crisis is the proximate trigger but the underlying mechanism is coalition-exhaustion-with-Netanyahu after 83 days of war-cabinet decision-making.

sources.name: Washington Post May 19 2026 - 'Senate advances resolution to block further strikes on Iran'; four Republicans + Democrats discharge from committee; Fetterman lone Democrat NO
MSNBC/MS.NOW May 19 2026 - 'Senate approves war powers vote to rein in Trump on Iran'
Common Dreams - Fetterman 'will not budge' baseline (Day 76 7th vote)

finding_cards.finding: Senate procedural movement: May 19 Senate voted to ADVANCE the war powers resolution by discharging it from committee. Four Republicans joined Democrats; Fetterman remained the lone Democrat NO. This is a procedural advance, not a passage on merits - the privileged-resolution vote is now scheduled but the underlying 49-50 arithmetic (Fetterman as decisive swing) is unchanged from Day 76 baseline absent further GOP defection or Fetterman pivot. The Day 81 SITREP's reading that postponement temporarily preserved the constitutional window holds: no AUMF; no judicial WPA challenge filed; Stage 2 hysteresis indicators (hostilities-terminated certification, Day 60 WPA deadline passed, operation-rebranding mechanism) continue to accumulate without active forcing function. The Vance May 19-20 statements ('locked and loaded' + 'pretty good spot') are administration-side dispositional signal not constitutional-architecture innovation. FT investigation: no new criminal referral signal. The procedural advance is a soft signal that congressional discipline is incrementally rising as the May 20-21 decision window operates; if Trump executes the scheduled-attack-rescheduling, the on-merits vote post-discharge becomes the operative forcing function rather than a future hypothetical.

The Fetterman baseline arithmetic holds; the procedural unlocking is incremental institutional pressure on the constitutional architecture absent any operational-vote-passage outcome. WPR-as-forcing-function status: ARMED but not FIRED.

finding_cards.conflict_notes: No conflict. Procedural advance is a routine step in the WPR cycle; whether on-merits passage occurs depends on Fetterman's position or further GOP defection. The signal is meaningful for Stage 2 hysteresis-tracking but does not fire the trigger.

finding_cards.probe_name: MOU Framework and Resumed-Operations Trigger Tracking

sources.name: TIME May 19 2026 - 'Trump Cancels Planned Attack on Iran, Citing Serious Negotiations'; Trump extends window: 'two or three days, maybe Friday, Saturday, Sunday, something, maybe early next week'
Vance May 19 2026 (Washington Times) - 'locked and loaded'; Iran faces 'two-way choice'
Vance May 20 2026 (Al Jazeera, The Hill) - 'pretty good spot here; lot of back-and-forth, lot of good progress is being made'
House of Commons Library May 2026 - Trump 'most points agreed to, but only point that really mattered, nuclear, was not'; Iran FM 'inches away' but criticizes 'maximalist demands'
Hot Air / Reuters digest May 19 - Iran 14-point text characterized in US framing as maximalist 'pay up and leave times five'

finding_cards.finding: Meta-Negotiation-Regression interruption from Day 81 continues but the substantive-convergence question remains unresolved. Trump (May 19) extended the 2-3 day window to 'Friday, Saturday, Sunday, something, maybe early next week' - the operative deal-or-rescheduling decision now lands approximately May 22-25, expanded from the Day 81 May 20-21 framing. Vance May 19 ('locked and loaded') and May 20 ('pretty good spot'; 'lot of good progress is being made') bracket the deal-direction read for the administration. Sticking point per Trump: 'most points agreed to, but the only point that really mattered, nuclear, was not.' Iran FM (Araghchi, per Commons Library) characterized US position as 'maximalist demands' but agreement 'inches away.' Iranian Pezeshkian X (May 19): 'dialogue does not mean surrender.' Pakistan-channel architecture remains operative; no 5th Oman round announced; mediator architecture: Pakistan-Qatar (trilateral bridge) primary, Oman dormant, Beijing dormant. US oil-sanctions waiver proposal still Tasnim-only on Iran side; US-side unconfirmed (Treasury action May 19 was Russian-crude tanker waiver, distinct policy line). Net: active diplomatic exchange continues; the structural sticking point is nuclear (HEU disposition) per Trump's own framing; the deal-very-close framing is sustained but the substantive gap is the same as Day 81. Window extension is a Gulf-state-brake-mechanism strength signal (brake holds; window extends) but also a no-resolution signal (substantive convergence absent).

Mediator architecture: Pakistan-Qatar bridge primary; Oman/Beijing dormant. Substantive nuclear gap unchanged. Meta-Negotiation-Regression interruption sustained; convergence absent.

finding_cards.conflict_notes: Vance hardline ('locked and loaded' May 19) vs Vance deal-direction ('pretty good spot' May 20): not contradiction; spans the cycle's mixed-signal range. The framework should price both as Vance principal-vertex activation rather than picking the deal-direction half. The Trump A1 pattern repeats at the Vance level: principal disposition is range-bound around the deal-window decision, not anchored at either pole.

sources.name: Fox News liveblog May 20 - Vahidi X post 'devastating blows' formulation
Pezeshkian X May 19 - 'dialogue does not mean surrender'
TIME May 19 - Trump 2-3 day window extended to Friday-Sunday early next week
Washington Times May 19 + Al Jazeera May 20 - Vance 'locked and loaded' + 'pretty good spot'
Trump Truth Social May 18 (T1 baseline) + Trump 'another big hit' rhetoric May 19

finding_cards.finding: FRAMEWORK REVISION TRIGGER. Multi-principal-vertex activation cycle: (1) VAHIDI direct named statement (Fox News May 20 X-attribution) - the framework's longest-tracked silent-apex-vertex breaks public silence; framing is kinetic-deterrence ('devastating blows that will leave you in abject defeat') not MOU-substantive (no 5-precondition restatement, no sunset-clause reference). The trigger fires on the silence-break itself per Day 81 PROBE-13 watch-signal. (2) PEZESHKIAN X (May 19) 'dialogue does not mean surrender' parallels the Day 81 Pezeshkian framing - civic-leader publicly defending diplomatic-engagement-as-non-retreat; complementary to Vahidi's deterrent-framing. (3) VANCE upgrade from Day 77 partial-fire to ACTIVE principal-vertex: May 19 'locked and loaded; two-way choice' + May 20 'pretty good spot; good progress' bracket the principal-disposition range. This is the first cycle Vance operates as live principal-vertex on the deal-direction question rather than as silent or marginal figure. (4) TRUMP extends his own deal-window (May 19): 'two or three days, maybe Friday, Saturday, Sunday, something, maybe early next week' - the Gulf-state-brake mechanism holds across extension and Trump A1-oscillation operates within extension framing. (5) Iranian asymmetric PA-gap structure: Vahidi (apex) speaks deterrent-public; Ghalibaf-Araghchi (mid-tier negotiating) operates Pakistan-channel; Pezeshkian (civic-presidential) frames diplomatic-engagement-as-non-retreat - three Iranian vertices in convergent voice on deterrence-with-engagement, against Day 81 read of 'private engagement + public deterrence' two-level pattern. The Vahidi statement is the apex-public-silence-break that the framework has been waiting on; the deliverable-to-the-MOU substantive endorsement gap remains.

sources.name: Washington Post May 19 2026 - 'UAE says drones that targeted Barakah nuclear power plant came from Iraqi territory'
Euronews May 19 2026 - 'UAE says mystery drones targeting nuclear plant came from Iraq'
Times of Israel May 17 2026 - 'Saudi Arabia says 3 drones launched from Iraqi airspace intercepted over kingdom' (concurrent Saudi airspace drone intercept; Iraqi-territory pattern)
Jerusalem Post May 18 (Day 81 baseline) - 'Iran ordered the attack' attribution by JPost sources

finding_cards.finding: FRAMEWORK REVISION TRIGGER. UAE OFFICIAL ATTRIBUTION (May 19): Barakah drones originated from IRAQI TERRITORY. This refines and complicates the Day 81 reading: the western-border launch pattern was reasonable inference toward Houthi (Yemen) or Iraqi militia; UAE-confirmed Iraqi territory consolidates the Iraqi-militia (Iranian-Quds-Force-aligned PMU) launch pathway over the Houthi alternative. Concurrent Saudi-airspace drone intercept (3 drones from Iraqi airspace May 17, Times of Israel) corroborates an Iraqi-territory launch corridor active across both Gulf-state targeting cycles. Implications: (a) Cooper Tier-1 SASC claim (May 14, 'Houthis cut off') is more accurate on the Houthi-supply-flow component but the operational-delivery capability traces to Iraqi PMU - a separate Iranian-extended-deterrent-network channel; Cooper's framing should be refined to 'Houthis-supply-cut, Iraqi-PMU-channel-operational' rather than blanket 'proxy connectivity cut.' (b) The Houthi-vs-Iraqi-militia attribution distinction is now operative; Houthi indigenous-manufacturing capability (FP March 2026 baseline) coexists with Iraqi PMU as a parallel Iranian-channel. (c) Iraqi-territory launch into both UAE (Barakah) and Saudi (intercepted) is structurally significant: it implicates Iranian Quds-Force coordination with Iraqi Shia militias - a more direct Iran-attribution pathway than Houthi-autonomous. The framework's BS-13 Iranian-residual-capability assessment elevates: not only is the network active (Day 81 finding) but the channel-architecture is now identified as Iraqi-PMU not Houthi-only. UAE has not formally attributed to Iran directly (territory-of-launch ≠ command-attribution); the operational-attribution gap remains.

trigger_detail.gap_to_full_match: UAE direct attribution to Iran (not only Iraqi territory); identification of specific Iraqi PMU faction (KH, AAH, NJBA, others) responsible; second-cycle Gulf-state-infrastructure attack confirming sustained-targeting pattern

BS-13 visibility 45-55% → 50-60%; channel-architecture: Iraqi-PMU operational, Houthi-supply-cut sustained, Hezbollah dormant, cyber Stage 3 latent. The framework's horizontal-escalation-ladder reading from Day 81 (nuclear-adjacent threshold crossed) is preserved; the channel-attribution refinement does not lower the threshold position.

finding_cards.conflict_notes: Cooper Tier-1 May 14 ('Houthis cut off from Iran's weapons') vs UAE May 19 attribution to Iraqi territory: not direct conflict; Cooper's framing was Houthi-focused; the Barakah delivery pathway is Iraqi-PMU not Houthi. Resolution: Cooper accurate on Houthi-supply-cut; Iraqi-PMU is a parallel Iranian-channel Cooper did not address. This refines rather than contradicts the Day 81 Cooper-discount; framework should preserve the discount on the broader proxy-connectivity claim while recognizing the Houthi-specific component may hold.

finding_cards.probe_name: Mutual Dispositional Reading and Power-Shift Perception

sources.name: Fox News May 20 - Vahidi 'devastating blows' framing; Mojtaba X 'Third Sacred Defense' framing (analyst-characterized as 'jihad')
Just the News May 20 - 'Mojtaba Khamenei declares victory in Third Sacred Defense of Iran'
PressTV May 15 - 'Leader: Epic of third sacred defense proof Shahnameh is Iran's reality' (Shahnameh/Ferdowsi civilizational frame; not Mahdist invocation)
TIME May 19 Trump 'might have to give Iran another big hit' + Vance May 19 'locked and loaded'
Vance May 20 'pretty good spot' + 'good progress'
Netanyahu absent at security consultations May 20 (IDF on 'high alert' Fox News May 19)

finding_cards.finding: Mojtaba X corpus (May 19-20) framing the war as 'Third Sacred Defense' / 'Sacred Defense against the American and Zionist invasion' (analyst-characterized as 'jihad' per Fox News). The framing draws on Shahnameh/Ferdowsi civilizational rhetoric ('Zahhak-likes', Iran-civilization-against-transgressors) and 'Sacred Defense' (Defah-e Moghadas) terminology established during the Iran-Iraq War, NOT Twelver Mahdist invocation (no Imam-al-Zaman language; no Hujjat-i-asr; no Mahdi-arrival framing per `eschatology-islam-v1.0.md` definition). The analyst 'jihad' label is single-source tier-3 interpretation. Per `eschatology-intersection-matrix.md` four-characteristic test: this is Tier-3 background indicator for PROBE-18 monitoring, NOT a Tier-1 BS-16 Mahdist trigger - the explicit non-fire is logged for framework discipline. Vahidi 'devastating blows' rhetoric (PROBE-2/PROBE-13 cross-ref) is hardline-deterrent at maximum. Trump 'another big hit' (May 19) + Vance 'locked and loaded' (May 19) at hardline pole; Vance 'pretty good spot' (May 20) at deal-direction pole - the cycle's US-side dispositional range remains paradox-state per Day 81 read. Netanyahu absent from Knesset dissolution vote (May 20) for 'security consultations' - dispositional signal of Iran-strike-readiness prioritized over coalition-discipline. IDF 'high alert' (Fox News May 19) holds. The Gulf-state-troika dispositional state from Day 81 (deal-seeking, hardened by Barakah; PROBE-20 territory) continues; the Iraqi-territory attribution may modestly compress Gulf-state alarm toward Iraq-channel-specific rather than Iranian-direct framing. No Smotrich/Ben-Gvir regime-nature breakout. No Mojtaba Mahdist-tier-1 invocation. Powell shifting-power mechanism continues to operate: Knesset dissolution passing elevates Israeli pre-emption incentive (electoral compression on coalition decision-window).

The framework non-fires BS-16 Tier-1 on this distinction; future Mojtaba statement on Hujjat-i-asr / Imam-al-Zaman / Mahdi-arrival would fire.

sources: Synthetic composite from PROBE-2, PROBE-9, PROBE-10, PROBE-12', PROBE-13, PROBE-14, PROBE-20 Day 83 outputs
finding_cards.finding: FRAMEWORK REVISION TRIGGER. Composite first-mover reading: the Day 81 'most compressed decision architecture' framing held but with structural movements at multiple thresholds. US-SIDE THRESHOLD: reached-and-braked (Day 81 baseline); window EXTENDED by Trump May 19 to 'Friday Saturday Sunday early next week' - Gulf-state-brake holds across one extension cycle (BS-18 durability +5pp; mechanism is not strictly 2-3 day expiry-and-trigger). IRANIAN-SIDE THRESHOLD: Vahidi public silence-broken - apex-vertex public statement on kinetic-deterrence is structurally significant for the first-mover question; Ghalibaf-Araghchi diplomatic-track operative via Pakistan-channel; no coalition rupture indicators (BS-1b held; rial baseline carry; no bazaar signal). ISRAELI-SIDE THRESHOLD: Knesset dissolution PRELIMINARY PASSED 110-0 (Day 82 framework-revision trigger fires); Netanyahu absent for 'security consultations' (Penetration-mechanism signal); pre-caretaker period operative; IDF 'high alert' Fox News May 19; no Israeli unilateral strike Day 82-83. WPR-FORCING-FUNCTION: procedural advance (committee discharge May 19) but on-merits vote not held; Fetterman-swing arithmetic unchanged. STRANGULATION: Brent $111.28 above-band sustained; no Kharg saturation; no commercial restoration. NEW ARCHITECTURE ELEMENT: Iraqi-PMU channel for Iranian-extended-deterrent (Barakah attribution UAE-confirmed May 19) is structurally significant - Iranian operational-delivery channel architecture clarified (Iraqi-PMU operative, Houthi-supply-cut sustained); the channel-architecture refinement affects first-mover signal-reading on Iranian-side (Iran can demonstrate kinetic threshold via Iraqi-PMU without firing Houthi mass-launch). COMPOSITE: the architecture is at maximum compression with extended-but-not-expanded window; the Knesset-passage trigger fires; the Vahidi silence-break fires; Iraqi-PMU channel-architecture refinement fires; the composite reading is 'three fires + Gulf-brake-extension' rather than 'window expiration + binary resolution.' The question 'which actor breaks first' has not resolved within the window; the window has extended; principal-vertex activation has saturated; the joint-distribution-of-thresholds remains operative.

Six-actor architecture: Iran, US, Israel, Gulf state troika (BS-18), Pakistan mediator (BS-11), Iraqi-PMU-channel (BS-13 attribution). Vance ACTIVE principal-vertex on deal-direction (PROBE-13). The joint-distribution-of-thresholds has multiple cell-fires without binary resolution; the operational reading is 'principal-vertex saturation with extended Gulf-state brake' rather than 'window expiration triggers fork resolution.'

The Day 83 reading sustains the Day 81 'compressed decision architecture' framing but the binary-resolution-within-window prediction was over-weighted; the actual pattern is 'principal-vertex saturation under extended brake' which is structurally different from 'imminent fork resolution.' Framework lesson: window-extensions are mechanism-state signals (brake durability), not resolution-failures; Fork D' marginally elevated as the window-extension itself becomes the operative state rather than the window-closure event.

finding_cards.probe_name: Gulf State Troika and Unaligned-Middle Pivot Capacity

sources.name: TIME May 19 - Trump credits 'Great Leaders and Allies' (MBS, MBZ, Tamim) for postponement; Gulf states stress 'positive momentum' in Pakistan-led mediation; Trump extends window to Friday-Sunday early next week
CBS News May 18 - Trump confirms hold at Gulf request; SecDef Hegseth/Joint Chiefs Cain instructed 'be prepared to go forward with a full, large scale assault on a moment's notice'
CNN May 19 - 'How Trump backed off on resuming Iran attacks - for now'
WaPo May 19 - UAE-official attribution: Barakah drones from Iraqi territory (NOT formal Iran-command attribution); UAE solidarity within Gulf-state framework holds
Al Jazeera May 18 - 'From Gulf states perspective, Hormuz reopening and Iran's missile programme are core issues' (Gulf-state-priority distinct from US nuclear-focus)

finding_cards.finding: Gulf-state troika brake mechanism HOLDS across one extension cycle. Trump (May 19) extended the 2-3 day window to 'Friday Saturday Sunday early next week' - the brake is durable beyond the original 2-3 day framing. Per Gulf-state messaging through Trump: 'positive momentum' in Pakistan-led mediation; deal 'will be made.' No public MBS, MBZ, or Tamim divergence; no intra-troika split signal. UAE-official attribution of Barakah drones to Iraqi territory (WaPo May 19) is structurally significant for BS-18: UAE did NOT escalate to direct Iran-command attribution despite being the Barakah target; the territory-not-command framing preserves UAE's diplomatic positioning within the troika brake mechanism rather than forcing Gulf-state hardening against Iran. No formal Qatar mediation-role announcement (Qatar continues dual role: Gulf brake actor via Emir Tamim + US-Israel diplomatic bridge via PM Mohammed bin Abdulrahman Al Thani). No formal Saudi-Iran principal-level meeting announcement (Hajj May 24-29 window approaches). No other unaligned-middle state (Turkey, India, Indonesia) joining mediation. Pakistan-channel architecture remains operative; Pakistan-Saudi-UAE-Qatar collective architecture sustained. From Gulf-state perspective: Hormuz reopening + Iran missile-programme are core issues (distinct from US nuclear-focus per Al Jazeera May 18) - Gulf-state-priority is regional-security-of-shipping-and-airspace, not US-priority-anti-proliferation; this priority-divergence is an unmodeled BS-18 dimension. Counter-vector mobilization: no public US §5.20 eschatological-coalition statement counter to Gulf-state brake; Smotrich/Ben-Gvir no public response; Israeli right-wing coalition operationally constrained by Knesset-dissolution stress. Brake-decay signals: no Trump expiration-of-patience statement; window-extension structurally INVERTS the expected decay pattern.

proposed_revision.edit: Update §5.25: Gulf-state troika brake mechanism DURABLE across one extension cycle. Original 2-3 day framing (May 20-21) extended by Trump May 19 to 'Friday Saturday Sunday early next week' (~May 22-25). Brake is not strictly one-off; mechanism withstands at least one extension under sustained Pakistan-mediation 'positive momentum' framing. BS-18 visibility 25-35% → 30-40%. Update Layer Three: operative forcing function is now Trump's extended window ~May 22-25, with Hajj (May 24-29) overlapping. Joint decision window: Knesset-dissolution-subsequent-readings × Trump extended window × Hajj overlap. The window-extension structurally inverts the expected decay pattern; the brake mechanism's durability is itself the cycle's structural signal. BS-15 first-mover architecture: six-actor (Iran, US, Israel, Gulf troika, Pakistan mediator, Iraqi-PMU channel-attribution per PROBE-14). The joint-distribution-of-thresholds has multiple cell-fires without binary fork-resolution.

The Day 77 partial-fire upgrades to ACTIVE principal-vertex on the deal-direction question. The Fetterman baseline arithmetic (49-50 against; Fetterman decisive) holds; the procedural unlocking is incremental institutional pressure on the constitutional architecture absent any operational-vote-passage outcome. proposed_revision.edit: Update PROBE-18 monitoring note: Mojtaba X corpus May 19-20 'Third Sacred Defense' / 'Sacred Defense against American and Zionist invasion' framing is Tier-3 background indicator (Mojtaba speech-corpus Mahdist-invocation frequency directive). EXPLICIT NON-FIRE for BS-16 Tier-1 (Mojtaba public Mahdist invocation in major speech): the source content draws on Defah-e Moghadas (Iran-Iraq War 'Sacred Defense' tradition) and Ferdowsi Shahnameh ('Zahhak-likes' framing; pre-Islamic Persian civilizational epic), NOT Twelver Mahdist eschatological tradition (no Imam-al-Zaman, no Hujjat-i-asr, no Mahdi-arrival language per `eschatology-islam-v1.0.md` definition). Analyst 'jihad' characterization (Fox News, single-source tier-3 interpretation) does not constitute eschatological-tradition activation per `eschatology-intersection-matrix.md` four-characteristic test. Framework discipline preserved by explicit non-fire log.

trigger_digest.appendix_b_row: PROBE-8 NEXT CYCLE: Brent above-band sustained ($111.28 close May 19); Day 81 reversal was intraday only; oscillation-band broken upward

proposed_revision.section: Section 2.2 Market Snapshot; Section 8 Probability Matrix oil-scenario probabilities
proposed_revision.edit: Update market snapshot: Brent closed $111.28 May 19, above the $102-110 A1-oscillation band; the Day 81 sweep's read of ~$102 intraday cluster did not session-close-durably reassert the band. Operating range is now $110-115 with kinetic-resumption premium sustained. Brent-through-$130-in-60-days: holds at 30-40% (Day 81 marker) pending May 20-25 extended window resolution; downside-to-Fork-B-trigger ($95) not approached. Framework lesson: distinguish intraday-deal-signal-spikes from session-close band-position; price the latter.

calibration_check.thesis_status: holding with structural elaboration on extended-brake mechanism and principal-vertex saturation
calibration_check.assessment: Central thesis holding. The materialist bargaining model continues to rank options under constraint surface; the Day 83 cycle adds three structural movements: (a) the Gulf-state troika brake mechanism is DURABLE across at least one extension cycle (not strictly one-off; the brake's decay-threshold is further out than the Day 81 original 2-3 day framing suggested); (b) the Vahidi apex-silence-break crystallizes the Iranian PA-gap as 'apex public-deterrent + mid-tier private-negotiator' two-level pattern rather than 'apex-veto-against-mid-tier' which was the BS-12 mechanism-revision read at Day 77; the apex consents to mid-tier negotiation activity by silence on substantive terms while publicly signaling kinetic-deterrence threshold; (c) the UAE-Iraqi-territory attribution refines BS-13 channel-architecture - Houthi-supply-cut framing (Cooper T1) holds on supply-component but Iraqi-PMU is a parallel Iranian-channel that Cooper's framing did not address. The materialist bargaining model correctly predicts that the Gulf-state-brake-mechanism's emergence-and-extension is the joint equilibrium of constrained choices (Iranian deterrence-signal activating Gulf-state economic-exposure activating Gulf-leader appeals activating Trump A1-oscillation) rather than designed-by-any-actor; the framework predicts the ranking, not the selection. The selection this cycle was 'Trump extends brake' rather than 'Trump expires brake into execution' - the ranking-prediction held; the selection was at the deal-direction pole of the ranked options.
calibration_check.dominant_uncertainty: Whether the extended window (~May 22-25) produces (a) substantive nuclear-gap convergence (Fork B-bilateral durability ↑), (b) further window-extension under sustained Gulf-state-brake (Fork D' extends), (c) Trump A1-oscillation back to execution after no-convergence (Fork A resumption), or (d) Israeli unilateral pre-emption under electoral-compression after Knesset-dissolution subsequent readings (Israeli variant ↑). The Vahidi MOU-substantive statement remains the most-consequential single signal; absent that, the Iranian-side ratification gap is not closed even with apex-silence-broken on deterrence-framing. The second dominant uncertainty: whether the Iraqi-PMU channel-architecture refinement produces second-cycle Gulf-state-infrastructure attack (sustained-targeting pattern would fire BS-13 escalation-ladder revision; absent recurrence the Day 81 nuclear-adjacent threshold is single-event).
